config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang adil dan tidak mengorbankan martabat atau kebebasan dasar seseorang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu-individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should come first, because in the United States we have seen too many examples of “the public good” being used as an excuse to trample minorities and unpopular people. Society does have real interests, like public safety, health, and basic order, but those interests should justify limits on rights only when the limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If you flip the priority and make society’s interests the default, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to silence dissent and call it justice. A healthy society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and then builds the common good around them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara yang berbeda, padahal justru perlindungan hak itulah yang mencegah kekuasaan sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi menurut saya harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keduanya secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my U.S. perspective, I am very wary of any argument that starts with “the common good” and ends up giving governments or majorities a blank check to punish dissent, minorities, or unpopular speech. Society can absolutely set narrow limits for safety, health, and order, but those limits need to be justified case by case, not assumed to override the person by default. In practice, protecting individual rights is usually what keeps society fair and prevents abuse of power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, padahal justru hak individu yang menjadi pagar agar mayoritas atau penguasa tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi menurut saya harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that framing makes me nervous because it is exactly how majorities and governments rationalize censorship, discrimination, and overreach while claiming they are serving the public good. Society absolutely has real claims on us, especially for safety and order, but those claims should be limited and tightly justified, not treated as automatically superior to a person’s basic freedoms. If individual rights are not the starting point, then “the interests of society” can become a convenient excuse for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
